hero.title: Still Running Your Business on Excel and Email?

offtheshelf.title: Off-the-Shelf Doesn't Fit
offtheshelf.description: You tried Salesforce or SAP but it's too complex, too expensive, or doesn't match your workflow.

start.title: Don't Know Where to Start

founder.intro: I'm Johnny Koo, founder of KooStory. I know what it's like to build a business from scratch and hit the wall where spreadsheets stop working.

startup.title: Scaled a Startup 0 to 270 Employees
startup.description: Built a Korean tech company to ~€25M revenue — I've lived the growing pains of a scaling business firsthand.

bootcamp.description: Founded a coding bootcamp — I know how to explain tech in simple words, without jargon.

requirements.title: I Build Requirements for You
requirements.description: My specialty: working with clients who don't know what to ask for. I translate your daily work into clear software specs.

berlin.title: Now in Berlin, Building for Germany
berlin.description: Bringing Korea's world-leading DX expertise to German Mittelstand companies.

workshop.title: Free Workshop (60 min)
workshop.description: You describe your daily work. No tech jargon. We listen.

document.title: Requirements Document
document.description: You get a clear User Story document in plain language describing exactly what to build.

build.title: We Build It
build.description: Small iterations, weekly demos. You see progress every week.

own.title: You Own It
own.description: Your software. Your data. Full handover, training, and support.

ai.title: AI Makes It Fast. We Make It Right.
ai.description: Yes, anyone can generate an app with AI in an afternoon. For a weekend project, that's great. But when your whole team depends on software every day — real customer data, real contracts, real money — you need more than a clever prompt.

architecture.title: Architecture, Not Just Code
architecture.description: AI generates functions. We design systems that scale with your business, integrate with your tools, and evolve as requirements change.

tested.title: Tested, Not Just Generated
tested.description: Every feature goes through automated tests, security reviews, and real-user validation before it touches your production environment.

owned.title: Owned, Not Rented
owned.description: Your codebase is clean, documented, and maintainable. No black boxes. Your next developer can read it, understand it, and extend it.

compliant.title: Compliant, Not Careless
compliant.description: GDPR, data residency, access controls, audit trails — the unsexy engineering that keeps your business legal and your customers' data safe.

korea.title: Built with Korean Engineering. Made for German Business.
korea.description: South Korea is the world's #1 digitized country. We bring that speed and digital-first thinking to your Mittelstand company.

checklist.title: Do You Really Need Custom Software?
checklist.intro: Before spending thousands, go through this honestly. Most of the time, the answer is not what you think.

step1.title: You probably don't need software at all.
step1.description: Most business problems can be solved with a well-structured spreadsheet, a better template, or a clearer process. If your workflow is messy, building software on top of it just automates the mess. Fix the process first.

step2.title: If you know the problem, you can build it yourself.
step2.description: If you're comfortable using a computer, you can build your own MVP or prototype with AI tools today. Search YouTube for 'vibe coding' — non-technical people are building their own software every day. Don't spend 20-30k EUR before trying this. Seriously, you can do it.
step2.workshop_before_link: If you want hands-on help: I run a 1-day 1:1 workshop (300 EUR). In Berlin I sit right next to you, online otherwise. You'll walk away with your own working program.

step3.title: If none of the above — let's build it together.
step3.description: You've tried spreadsheets, you've tried building yourself, and the problem is genuinely complex enough for custom software. That's where we come in.

workshop.title: Let's Figure Out What You Actually Need
workshop.description: Book a free 60-minute requirements workshop. You'll walk away with a draft requirements document — whether you work with us or not.

deliverables.processmap: Process map of your current workflow

workshop.trust: No commitment. No tech jargon. Just a conversation about your business.

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.description: Have questions? Want to discuss your project? Drop us a message.

form.name: Name
form.messagePlaceholder: Tell us about your business challenge...

seo.title: Ilmo Koo — What I Do
seo.description: Real examples of business problems I have solved. Requirements workshops, process automation, custom software. Founder of Kooslab (Seoul) and KooStory (Berlin).

hero.title: Here's what I do — with real examples.
hero.subtitle: I'm Ilmo Koo, founder of Kooslab (Seoul) and KooStory (Berlin). When a partner asks "what can you actually do?", I'd rather show than tell.

cases.title: Real Problems I've Solved
cases.intro: Every project below started with a business problem, not a feature request. Here's what the customer was struggling with, what I did, and what changed.

groupA.title: I defined it — so someone else could build it right
groupA.intro: Requirements consulting projects where I created the requirements document, and the client used it to successfully outsource to a dev agency.

groupB.title: I built it
groupB.intro: End-to-end projects where I defined AND built the solution.

safety.label: Software Requirements
safety.pain: Had deep domain expertise in safety solutions but no idea how to turn it into a software product. Didn't know what to ask for or how to brief developers.
safety.did: Ran requirements workshops, wrote detailed user stories, created wireframes and process maps. Discovered features and pages the client hadn't even considered.
safety.result: Result: Client got a clear development roadmap and requirements document. Successfully outsourced development to an agency with zero confusion.

onui.label: CRM Software Requirements
onui.pain: Needed a custom CRM but couldn't articulate what they needed. Previous attempts with dev agencies failed because requirements were unclear.
onui.did: Consulted on the full CRM requirements — workflows, data models, user stories. Created a comprehensive requirements document.
onui.result: Result: Successfully outsourced to a dev agency with a clear brief. No back-and-forth, no scope creep.

mindlogic.label: SSO Integration Requirements
mindlogic.pain: Their AI product (FactChat, similar to ChatGPT) needed to integrate with each university's own authentication system (SSO). Every university had a different login system.
mindlogic.did: Created an improved client requirements questionnaire that standardized the information gathering across universities.
mindlogic.result: Result: Made the SSO integration project 50% faster by eliminating back-and-forth communication. One questionnaire, clear answers, straight to implementation.

finnest.label: Finance Automation
finnest.pain: 100% of financial data lived in Excel spreadsheets with complicated formulas. People edited and broke formulas. Hard to operate, hard to scale. Getting data from banks and card companies was manual.
finnest.did: Built automated data extraction/scraping that runs every 24 hours, pulling data from banks and card systems. Integrated the extracted data into live operational spreadsheets with their existing complex formulas. Used n8n for workflow automation.
finnest.result: Result: Eliminated daily manual data entry. Financial operations run automatically with reliable data.

workspace.label: Shared Office Operations
workspace.pain: Shared office in Seoul managed all contracts and payments the traditional way — manual spreadsheet work for everything.
workspace.did: Built custom subscription payment and contract management software with e-signature for online contract signing.
workspace.result: Result: 90% of manual operations eliminated. Contracts, billing, and payments now automated.

grocery.label: Delivery Kiosk
grocery.pain: At a major grocery department in Seoul, customers wrote their delivery addresses by hand. Slow process (60–100 seconds per order), required a dedicated staff member.
grocery.did: Built a touch-based kiosk connected to a logistics API with a favorite address feature. Customers enter their mobile number, load their saved address, and complete the order.
grocery.result: Result: Order time dropped from 60–100 seconds to 10 seconds. Eliminated one full-time hire — customers now self-serve.

tour.label: Tour Booking Platform
tour.pain: Tour guides in Berlin and across Europe managed private tours completely by hand — bookings, payments, communication, everything manual.
tour.did: Built a platform where tour guides upload their tour products online. Travelers can browse, pay, and reserve online, with automatic messenger notifications.
tour.result: Result: Tour guides went from manual operations to an automated booking and payment system.

parking.label: LPR Integration
parking.pain: Client wanted a CCTV-based AI parking management system but needed License Plate Recognition integrated into their existing cloud-based parking software.
parking.did: Integrated a License Plate Recognition (LPR) module into their cloud-based parking management software.
parking.result: Result: Automated vehicle identification and parking management through CCTV.

howIWork.title: How I Work
howIWork.p1: Partner, not vendor. I stay until it works for your business. If something needs to change after delivery, we change it.
howIWork.p2: Flexible and lightweight. I act as your tech team. No big agency overhead, no months of onboarding.
howIWork.p3: I'd rather build one thing you use daily than ten features that collect dust. If a feature turns out to be useless, we cut it. Your success is my metric.

about.title: About Me
about.p1: Engineer by training — aerospace engineering at the University of Maryland. Requirements and specs since day one: Korean Air (flight control systems), Vector Korea (automotive embedded software). Scaled Code States to 200+ team, $27M revenue as CPO — automated everything because growth demanded it.
about.p3: Seoul + Berlin. Korean native, English business-level, German in progress.

wontDo.title: What I Won't Do
wontDo.p1: I politely decline building software nobody will use.
wontDo.p2: If your idea doesn't solve a real daily problem, I'll tell you honestly.
wontDo.p3: Pretty code that creates no business value is waste.
wontDo.p4: Sometimes the best advice is "don't build that."

workshops.title: Workshops
workshops.intro: Whether you need clarity on what to build, or want to learn to build it yourself.

free.title: Requirements Consulting Workshop
free.description: 120 min, online or offline. We go through your business problem and you walk away with a clear picture of what to build. No commitment.

requirements.title: 1-Day Requirements Workshop
requirements.description: Full day, deep dive. You get a complete requirements document — user stories, process maps, priorities. Hand it to any dev team.

vibe.title: 1-Day Vibe Coding Workshop
vibe.description: Hands-on 1:1. I sit next to you (Berlin offline, otherwise online) and you build your own MVP or prototype by the end of the day.

paidLink.title: Paid workshops — early bird pricing available
paidLink.description: Requirements Workshop (200 EUR) and Vibe Coding Workshop (100 EUR). 50% off until April 30, 2026.

workshops.priceNote: Prices will rise soon. Book now to lock in the current rate.

seo.title: Workshops — KooStory
seo.description: 1-day workshops for business owners. Requirements workshop or vibe coding workshop. Early bird pricing until April 30, 2026.

hero.title: Workshops for Business Owners
hero.subtitle: Whether you need someone to define what to build, or you want to learn to build it yourself. One day, clear results.

requirements.title: Requirements Workshop
requirements.description: A full day dedicated to your business problem. You walk away with a complete requirements document that any development team can work from.

vibe.title: Vibe Coding Workshop
vibe.description: You build your own MVP or prototype by end of day. No coding experience needed — if you can use a computer, you can do this.
includes: Set up your AI coding environment
Learn to iterate and improve on your own
Your own deployed application by end of day
vibe.format: Berlin (I sit right next to you) or online

free.title: Free Requirements Consulting
free.description: 120-minute session, online or offline. We go through your business problem and you get a clear picture of what to build. No strings attached.

workshops.priceNote: Early bird pricing ends April 30, 2026. After that, regular prices apply (400 EUR / 200 EUR).

items.a: No. The requirements workshop is designed for business owners who know their problem but not the technical solution. The vibe coding workshop teaches you from zero — if you can use email, you can do this.

items.q: What if I'm not in Berlin?
items.a: Both workshops work perfectly online. For the vibe coding workshop, I share my screen and guide you step by step.

items.q: What do I get at the end?
items.a: Requirements workshop: a complete document you can hand to any developer. Vibe coding workshop: a working application you built yourself, deployed and accessible.
Yes — and it's a great combination. Define what to build first, then build the MVP yourself. Contact me for a combined rate.
